steps.title: Simplify both sides to standard form
steps.content:
Expand the left side: $\frac{12x+28}{4} = 3x + 7$, so the equation becomes:
$$3x + 7 - \frac{s}{13} = rx - 8r$$
Both sides are now linear in $x$.

steps.title: Apply the infinitely many solutions condition
steps.content:
A linear equation has infinitely many solutions only if both sides are identical—the coefficient of $x$ must match on both sides, and the constant term must match.

Coefficient of $x$: $3 = r$, so $r = 3$
Constant terms: $7 - \frac{s}{13} = -8r$

steps.title: Substitute r = 3 and solve for s
